name: Code Janitor
summary: An always-on agent that opens small, safe cleanup PRs behind your CI gates, with a very high merge rate.
prerequisites: GitHub connected, with push access to the target repository and permission to open PRs.
Slack (optional) for notifications when a PR is opened.
An issue tracker (optional, e.g. Linear/Jira/GitHub Issues) if you want it to pull "easy" tracked issues.
This workflow depends on an external memory store. Because it is an engineering workflow, we recommend a GitHub repository as that memory.
Create a new GitHub repository (private is fine) and seed it from the [memory template](https://github.com/adaptcom/workflows/tree/main/workflows/code-janitor/memory-template).
Adapt uses that repository as its source of truth to remember what it has already done and continuously improve over time.
